_comment: Penn build-mode model routing. Cheap-by-default (Haiku); a task class or a runtime signal forces smart (Sonnet). This is the SOURCE OF TRUTH read by penn-router.ts. It is only LAW where something reads it and refuses — see _enforcement. tiers values are OpenClaw /model TOKENS ('sonnet' | 'haiku'), NOT API model strings — unlike Flock's routing-policy.json. Relaxing a class is a money-path decision: review before merging.

render.rationale: Deterministic Sharp compositing; capability proven in the spike. Risk is implementation bugs, not discovery. Gate with proof, not compute.

host_validation.rationale: Order advancement, money, and state. A careless edit stalls or mis-advances an order SILENTLY. Same family as the payment path. Start smart; do not let Haiku attempt-then-break.

failure_rescue.when: same step fails N times | tests still red after K attempts | edit reverted | command error repeats
failure_rescue.prompt: Escalation. Re-check the last step against LIVE state before continuing. The reasoning carried in this thread is what drifted — question it, do not assume it is correct. Audit before acting.
failure_rescue.note: The in-thread /model switch carries context. Here that is a LIABILITY, not an asset: the carried reasoning is the thing that went off course. The prompt forces a re-examination instead of a continuation.

self_request.note: Penn asking to escalate is honored — cheap to do. But it is ONE input, not the gate: a drifting model does not know it is drifting, which is why the file-class and failure-loop triggers exist above it.

stickiness.note: render is a HARD cheap floor that breaks the latch on purpose — crossing from gate-wiring into compositor rendering is a clean boundary, and the savings live there.

compositor_gate.rule: A change to action-applier.ts case 'request_mockup' and a change to sharp-compositor.ts MUST land in the same commit. Never split.
compositor_gate.alsoInThisChange: Resolve or formally dismiss the 4->2 variant:'both' bug (emits A,A_alt,B,B_alt; should emit A,B). Sonnet decides live-or-moot in this same pass.
compositor_gate.enforcedBy: scripts/precommit-coupling.sh (git pre-commit hook) — independent of the harness, works now.

_enforcement.file_class_tier: STRONGEST but CONDITIONAL: a harness pre-tool-use hook reads this file; if a write targets a host_validation glob while the session tier is cheap, it BLOCKS or forces `/model sonnet`. Requires OpenClaw to support a pre-tool hook — confirm. Without it, this layer degrades to 'Penn reads the policy and is told to obey', which is the drift you are trying to kill.
_enforcement.coupling: git pre-commit hook (scripts/precommit-coupling.sh). Real law, no harness dependency. Only fires where commits happen with the hook installed — if you SFTP files to the droplet without committing there, it does NOT fire.
_enforcement.review_before_live: deploy guard: a host_validation change must not reach `systemctl restart` of the live host without a review flag. Implement at the deploy script; mechanics depend on how you push to the box.
